# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM debian:latest

# Update and install necessary packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    apache2 \
    libapache2-mod-php \
    php-pgsql \
    python3 \
    python3-flask-api \
    postgresql \
    redis-server \
    curl

# Copy necessary files
COPY . /var/www/html

# Expose necessary ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Configure apache
RUN chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/html/ && a2enmod rewrite

# Configure postgresql
USER postgres
RUN service postgresql start &&\
    psql --command "CREATE USER dbuser WITH SUPERUSER PASSWORD 'dbuser';" &&\
    createdb -O dbuser dbname &&\
    service postgresql stop

USER root
RUN echo "localhost:5432:*:dbuser:dbuser" > /root/.pgpass
RUN chmod 600 /root/.pgpass

# Configure services
COPY apache.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf
COPY api_server.py /usr/local/bin/
COPY db_config.conf /etc/postgresql/12/main/
COPY redis.conf /etc/redis/

# Make sure services start on run
RUN echo "/etc/init.d/apache2 start" >> /root/.bashrc
RUN echo "python3 /usr/local/bin/api_server.py &" >> /root/.bashrc
RUN echo "/etc/init.d/postgresql start" >> /root/.bashrc
RUN echo "/usr/bin/redis-server /etc/redis/redis.conf &" >> /root/.bashrc

CMD ["/bin/bash"]
